title: Data Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Data Analyst – Technology Consulting (Assistant Manager / Manager) **Sector:** Professional Services – Consulting **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid) **Salary:** €60,000 – €80,000 \+ bonus \& benefits **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded, market\-leading professional services firm with a rapidly expanding Technology Consulting practice. Operating on a global scale, they partner with a diverse range of clients to deliver data\-driven solutions and transformation initiatives. The firm is known for its collaborative culture, strong leadership and clear progression pathways, making it an excellent platform for career growth. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity to join a high\-growth consulting team working on impactful, data\-led projects across multiple industries. You will gain exposure to senior stakeholders, work on complex transformation initiatives and play a key role in shaping how organisations use data to drive decision\-making. The team offers a strong blend of technical and commercial exposure, along with clear progression opportunities into leadership roles. You will be working in a collaborative, forward\-thinking environment with access to cutting\-edge tools and technologies. **Who should apply?** This role will suit candidates currently working in data analytics, consulting or industry roles who are looking to take the next step in a client\-facing, project\-driven environment. * 3–8 years’ experience in data analytics or related roles * Strong experience with data visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau or Excel * Proficiency in SQL, Python or similar programming languages * Experience working with large datasets, data transformation and data quality processes * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Experience in consulting or client\-facing environments is advantageous **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting into senior leadership within the Technology Consulting team, this role will involve: * Analytics Delivery – Supporting end\-to\-end data analytics engagements across multiple clients * Data Transformation – Cleaning, validating and transforming data to ensure accuracy and usability * Visualisation – Developing dashboards and reports to communicate key insights * Stakeholder Engagement – Working with clients to gather requirements and deliver actionable insights * Automation – Supporting process improvement initiatives using tools such as Power Query and Power Automate * Documentation – Producing technical documentation and process workflows * Project Delivery – Managing multiple workstreams and ensuring timely delivery of outputs * Innovation – Staying up to date with emerging trends in analytics and applying new tools and techniques **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Frank Bailey who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200

title: Data Analyst (Financial),
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** Serving virtually every dimension of the health system, we work with a diverse set of clients across 150 countries \- from those who diagnose and treat patients to those who pay for care, deliver health services, and those who supply the cures. Optum maintains operations across North America, South America, Europe, Asia Pacific and the Middle East. Our innovative partnerships provide technology and tools that enable unprecedented collaboration and efficiency. As a result, we can tap into valuable health care data to uncover insights and develop strategies for better care at lower costs. **About The Role** As our **Data Analyst** you will join an established team within OptumRx responsible for managing our network pharmacy pricing guarantees. You will provide financial modelling and analytics related to the performance of effective rate pricing guarantees, specific to the Commercial business. You will work with internal stakeholders to drive to best outcomes and ensure alignment with our pharmacy contractual obligations. Within the role, you will build relationships with our partners and stakeholders across many functions, including Network Contracting, Client Contract Management, Finance, Legal and more. We're looking for someone with a quantitative mindset, attention to detail, a solid aptitude for complex problem solving, industry experience, and well\-developed communication and technical skills. Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin or Letterkenny office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **Primary Responsibilities** * Support the monitoring and reporting of pricing guarantees for network pharmacies within Optum's Commercial book of business * Produce and maintain financial models and projections, ensuring inputs, assumptions, and calculations are accurate, well documented, and reviewed regularly * Collaborate with internal partners to gather required inputs and ensure models reflect contractual and operational considerations, maintaining a solid focus on precision and consistency in results * Identify data issues, emerging trends, or potential risks and escalate appropriately with clear supporting analysis * Communicate findings clearly through written summaries, reports, and presentations tailored to internal stakeholders You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications** * Proven solid experience with SQL and/or Python, with the ability to perform effective and efficient querying involving multiple tables and subqueries * Demonstrated communication skills, able to work with stakeholders from multiple levels and disciplines **Preferred Qualifications** * Proven hands\-on experience with MS Azure * Proven PBM or Pharma experience * Proven experience in financial modelling * Proven ability to be comfortable analyzing and interpreting large volumes of data * Demonstrated ability to understand complex scenarios and quickly provide effective decisions **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA

title: Data Architect
description: **Deloitte is the biggest professional services Firm in the world and making an impact is more than just what we do: it’s why we’re here. We’re driven to create positive progress for our clients, community, people, and the planet. This sense of purpose inspires us to work to the highest standards, to tackle the challenges that matter.***Joining us means becoming part of a Consulting team that’s at the top of its game and growing. Working with the best people and technology in the market, you’ll tackle meaningful projects that transform the future for high\-profile local and international clients. And you’ll do it as part of a people\-first culture that’s supportive, collaborative and, above all, welcoming.* . At Deloitte, our Artificial Intelligence \& Data Team drives innovation by transforming data into actionable insights that empower our clients. As a Data Architect, you will play a pivotal role in designing and delivering cutting\-edge data solutions across diverse industries, leveraging the latest technologies and our global network. This role offers a unique opportunity to lead impactful projects, collaborate cross\-functionally, and develop your leadership skills in a dynamic, supportive environment focused on continuous learning and personal growth.**Key skills and prior experience*** A minimum of a third\-level degree in Maths, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline * Proven experience leading and delivering complex data\-related projects, with the ability to motivate and guide teams to achieve outstanding results * Strong stakeholder management skills, including influencing senior leaders and providing expert advice based on industry best practices * Commercial experience in information and data management, with familiarity in enterprise\-grade data architecture technologies such as Cloudera CDP, Azure Databricks, and cloud\-native environments **What type of work will I be doing?*** Scoping, designing, and leading the implementation of scalable data solutions and models using open source and proprietary technologies * Developing technical specifications and architecture designs for data storage and management aligned with enterprise standards * Ensuring data security and integrity are embedded within all architectural designs * Leading and mentoring data analysts and engineers to deliver solutions aligned with target architectures * Collaborating across Deloitte’s Technology and Transformation business areas to integrate data capabilities with broader organisational goals * Managing client engagements across various industries, building trusted relationships and identifying growth opportunities * Coaching junior team members, aligning their responsibilities with their professional development objectives **Who is the Hiring Manager?** The hiring manager is an experienced leader within Deloitte’s Artificial Intelligence \& Data Team, known for a collaborative and inclusive leadership style. They value open communication, professional development, and fostering a positive team culture. Their approach balances high performance expectations with empathy, encouraging innovation and continuous learning while maintaining a strong focus on delivering exceptional client outcomes.**Where is this role based?** This role is based in Dublin or Cork, with a flexible working model that includes hybrid arrangements between office and client sites. For more information, please visit our Deloitte Works webpage.*If you believe that you meet most of the criteria above, we encourage you to apply. Deloitte is an equal opportunities employer and will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process.* **What we offer** *Your reward at Deloitte is competitive, supports our purpose and enables our people to never stop growing. Your reward at Deloitte is not just your salary, we believe in investing in our people's personal and professional development and empowering their work\-life balance. Our ambition is to achieve gender equality and enable continuous growth. We offer supports and benefits that suit you, wherever you are in life, including health and well\-being, pension, savings, training, coaching and* enhanced leave options *among others.**Keep on being you. Bring your full, absolute self to work, every day. Explore, question and collaborate. Stretch your thinking, while building a career that inspires and energises you. And, whatever motivates you, keep growing both professionally and personally – because when you make an impact that matters, we do too.*

title: Data Engineer
description: **The Central Bank of Ireland serves the public interest by safeguarding monetary and financial stability and by working to ensure that the financial system operates in the best interests of consumers and the wider economy.** We currently have a vacancy for an **Associate Data Analyst, People Analytics** on a **specified purpose contract of 12 months** in the **HR Division** . This role is aligned with the **Bank Executive grade** and the BEX salary scale of *€39,090* applies. Please click here for further information on our salary scales. The purpose of the role is to assist the People Analytics team with specialist data analytics expertise to support the delivery of effective outcomes, including the production of relevant periodic management information. The role requires a comprehensive understanding of statistical analysis, business analytics, and practical experience in leveraging diverse data analytics tool to drive informed decision\-making within People Analytics. **Responsibilities** * Assist in delivering comprehensive data analysis findings, supporting divisional actions via descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and decision analytics. * Prepare and analyse information ensuring timely, accurate delivery to stakeholders, effectively communicating completed work outcomes. * Continuously design, implement, and enhance reporting capabilities and tools to produce valuable management insights for informed business decisions. * Ensure stringent data integrity controls and compliance with relevant procedures, policies, and legislative requirements. * Collaborate with colleagues to facilitate effective project and work program delivery within the People Analytics domain. **Requirements** * Hold a third level honours degree or professional qualification in statistics, business analytics, computer science, mathematics, finance, economics, risk, or related fields. * 3\-5 years of relevant experience in data analytics within industry, academia, or financial services, spanning data science, statistical consulting, business intelligence, or data\-focused risk analysis. * Possess understanding/experience in applying data science/analytics in a business context along with practical knowledge of analytics software/tools and infrastructure. * Exhibit proficiency in database management (SQL, SQL Server/Services), statistical analysis tools (R, SAS, Matlab), programming languages (Python, Java, Julia), and business intelligence reporting/visualisation tools. Ability to acquire proficiency in new tools/languages as required. * Willingness to understand the broader regulatory environment, coupled with strong communication, organisational, workload management, judgment, and professional conduct skills. We know it's our people who make the Central Bank special and we are focused on creating a diverse, inclusive, fulfilling and progressive work environment. We encourage applications from candidates with different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives as it strengthens us, as individuals and as an organisation. If we can make any reasonable accommodations for you in the recruitment process in order to give you the opportunity to perform to your best, please email our Disability Inclusion Partner, Aobh Kavanagh at aobh.kavanagh@centralbank.ie. Any information that you provide will be used only for the purposes of providing relevant support and will have no bearing on how your application will be viewed. *We have recently implemented a hybrid working model to balance the flexibility working from home provides with the value our office environments bring to support collaboration and connection with colleagues.* **Our approach to hybrid working enables colleagues to work from home up to 50% of working days, building on an existing broad range of flexible working policies and practices already in place to support our people achieve the right balance.** Our policies also provide insight into our organisational culture, work environment and working arrangements. Before starting your application you will be asked to create a profile with us, this will allow you to track and review your application throughout the process. Click "register" to create a profile and complete the application process. Once your application has been successfully submitted you will receive an automatic email from us acknowledging receipt. If you do not receive this auto\-acknowledgement, please contact recruitment@centralbank.ie Our policies also provide insight into our organisational culture, work environment and working arrangements. Details of the appropriate pension scheme will be provided upon determination of the appointee’s status.** *Cuirfear fáilte roimh iarratais i nGaeilge* *The Central Bank of Ireland is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Development Officer (Schools & Community Development)
description: Fencing Ireland serves as the national governing body for the Olympic sport of fencing in Ireland. Dedicated to fostering the sport's growth, we support athletes, coaches, and clubs across the country. Fencing Ireland is seeking a proactive and energetic Development Officer to deliver schools programmes and support the growth of fencing at community level, particularly within DEIS areas. Reporting to the Head of Development, this is a hands\-on role focused on introducing fencing to new participants, building relationships, and creating pathways from schools into clubs, while ensuring high\-quality programme delivery that supports ongoing engagement and retention. **What We’re Looking For** We are looking for someone with the right personality, energy, and initiative to grow fencing on the ground. You do not need to be an elite fencer or highly experienced coach. Training and support will be provided. What matters most is your ability to connect with people, build relationships, and turn opportunities into active programmes. **Key Responsibilities** **Schools Programme Delivery** * Deliver fencing sessions in DEIS schools as part of a funded development programme * Lead Transition Year (TY) programmes, introducing students to fencing in an engaging and structured way * Deliver primary school programmes over multi\-week blocks * Ensure an engaging experience for schools to support long\-term partnerships **Community \& Club Development** * Work with local stakeholders (schools, community groups, sports partnerships) to develop fencing clubs in local communities close to the schools * Support the establishment of new fencing clubs in target areas * Deliver evening sessions (approx. 2–3 per week) to support club activity * Create pathways from school programmes into clubs, supporting long\-term growth **Equipment \& Operations** * Transport fencing equipment to schools and venues (vehicle provided) * Support organisation and maintenance of equipment * Ensure safe setup and use of equipment **Events \& Federation Support** * Support the delivery of Fencing Ireland competitions and events (approx. 5–6 weekends per year) * Assist with event setup and breakdown * Act as a representative of Fencing Ireland **Working Hours** * Full\-time, 1 Year Contract with potential to renew * Busier during the 9\-month fencing season, with more flexibility during quieter periods * Includes evening and weekend work * This is not a standard 9–5 role and requires flexibility **Candidate Profile** **Essential** * Some fencing experience * Strong interpersonal and communication skills * Confidence working with young people and community groups * Self\-starter with ability to take initiative * Ability to build and maintain strong relationships with schools and stakeholders * Full clean driving licence * Willingness to travel regularly (Dublin / Leinster) * Flexibility * Willingness to undergo Garda Vetting * Willingness to complete further training (safeguarding, coaching, etc.) A coaching qualification, either in fencing or another sport, is desirable but not essential as full training will be provided. Experience in teaching or youth work, and in community or sports development is a plus. Salary depending on experience \- €30,000 \- €34,000

title: ECM Seller Engagement & C2C Associate
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. **About The Team And The Role** The ECM Seller Engagement team partners with B2C and C2C sellers across ECM markets to strengthen relationships, support growth strategies, and build a connected seller community. Based in Dublin, this team works cross\-functionally to deliver clear communications, impactful programs, and high\-quality events that support seller success and business performance. As an ECM Seller Engagement \& C2C Junior Associate, you will support seller communication strategies, contribute to C2C growth initiatives, and lead the execution of internal and external events. This role offers exposure to cross\-market collaboration and strategic initiatives, with opportunities to develop project management, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills. **What You Will Accomplish** * Support the development and execution of communication strategies for B2C and C2C sellers, ensuring clear, consistent, and engaging messaging across channels. * Plan and deliver end\-to\-end ECM events, including internal events and external trade fairs, ensuring seamless execution and positive participant experiences. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams and external partners to bring seller\-focused initiatives and programs to life. * Contribute to the execution of the C2C growth strategy across ECM markets, supporting initiatives that increase traffic, supply, and sales. * Monitor performance metrics and assist in implementing actions that drive measurable business impact. * Strengthen seller engagement by delivering programs and communications that inform, inspire, and build community. **What You Will Bring** * Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects and priorities simultaneously. * Clear and effective written and verbal communication skills. * A collaborative approach and proactive mentality, with the ability to work across teams and markets. * Attention to detail combined with a hands\-on, execution\-focused work style. * Interest in e\-commerce, seller communities, and marketplace environments. * Based in or willing to work in Dublin, with the ability to support in\-person events as required. **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Electrical Engineer - Electrical Design & Schematic Drafting
description: Cental is a global supplier of fully designed, engineered, built and integrated modular and skid\-mounted solutions for the Data Centre, Utilities, Life Sciences, and Oil \& Gas markets. From our headquarters in Carlow, Ireland, we deliver comprehensive solutions for clients worldwide. Our people are the foundation of our success. With three manufacturing facilities and a talented team of engineers, designers, project managers, and tradespeople, our team manages every detail, ensuring seamless delivery from initial design to final installation. We value teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, creating a collaborative, innovative environment where everyone can reach their potential. We also believe in celebrating achievements and recognising effort. From monthly quality and safety initiatives for breakfast treats and local tool vouchers, to company breakfasts, staff BBQs, and occasional outings, we bring people together to enjoy achievements and strengthen our team spirit. Growth and learning are central to Cental. Whether you’re joining as an apprentice, graduate, or experienced professional, we provide training and development opportunities to help you build a rewarding, long\-term career. If you’re passionate about excellence, innovation, and teamwork, join us and be part of a company that’s engineering solutions and building futures. **Position Overview** We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Electrical Engineer with a strong background in electrical design and schematic drafting to join our engineering team. The successful candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and reviewing electrical systems and schematics for a range of industrial and commercial projects. This role requires precision, creativity, and strong technical expertise in electrical engineering principles, CAD\-based design, and compliance with industry standards. **Key Responsibilities** ****Electrical Design \& Drafting:**** * Develop detailed electrical designs and schematics for power distribution, control systems, and automation projects. * Produce accurate wiring diagrams, single\-line diagrams, and control panel layouts. * Use industry\-standard CAD/ECAD tools (e.g., AutoCAD Electrical, EPLAN, or similar) to create and update technical drawings. * Ensure designs meet project requirements, client specifications, and applicable codes and standards. **Technical Engineering Duties** * Perform electrical load calculations, cable sizing, and equipment selection. * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to integrate electrical systems with mechanical, civil, and architectural designs. * Conduct design reviews and implement revisions based on feedback from clients, engineers, and project managers. * Provide technical input during procurement, manufacturing, and installation stages. **Project Management \& Collaboration** * Liaise with clients, suppliers, and contractors to clarify technical requirements. * Support project scheduling, cost estimation, and material selection for electrical works. * Ensure timely delivery of design documentation in line with project milestones. **Compliance \& Quality** * Ensure all designs comply with relevant IEC, BS, or other applicable standards. * Conduct design risk assessments and adhere to safety regulations. * Maintain quality control processes for drawings and documentation. **Essential** ****Qualifications \& Skills**** * Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering or related discipline. * 5–10 years of professional experience in electrical design and schematic drafting. * Proficiency in AutoCAD Electrical, EPLAN, or equivalent ECAD software. * Strong knowledge of electrical engineering principles, power distribution, control systems, and automation design. * Experience with electrical calculations, cable sizing, and equipment selection. * Understanding of relevant electrical standards (IEC, BS, EN, etc.). * Strong attention to detail and accuracy in design documentation. **Desirable** * Chartered Engineer status (or working towards it). * Experience in low voltage (LV) and extra\-low voltage (ELV) systems. * Knowledge of PLC/automation integration in electrical designs. * Experience with BIM/Revit MEP for electrical services. **Personal Attributes** * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Endpoint Management Specialist
description: **Location:** Dublin, Leinster, IE **Job Type:** Full\-time **Work Type:** On\-site / Hybrid (specify as needed) **Role Type:** Professional / Technical **Overview of Role** We are seeking an experienced Endpoint Management Specialist with deep expertise in Microsoft Intune and Microsoft Configuration Manager (SCCM). This role is central to ensuring secure, dependable, and scalable management of our customer environments. The ideal candidate combines strong technical capability with a strategic mindset, enabling modern device management while supporting legacy environments during transition. **Role \& Responsibilities:** * Design, implement, and maintain Microsoft Intune and Configuration Manager environments. * Manage the full life\-cycle of Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android devices using modern management principles. * Develop and maintain device compliance, configuration, and security policies. * Oversee application deployment, updates, and patch management across all managed endpoints. * Maintain and optimise co\-management or tenant\-attach configurations where applicable. * Implement and enforce endpoint security baselines, conditional access policies, and Zero Trust\-aligned controls. * Collaborate with security teams to ensure device posture meets organisational and regulatory requirements. * Monitor and remediate vulnerabilities through integrated tools such as Defender for Endpoint. * Create and maintain automation scripts (PowerShell preferred) to streamline endpoint operations. * Improve deployment processes, device provisioning, and user on\-boarding through Autopilot and related technologies. * Continuously evaluate and enhance endpoint management processes for efficiency and reliability. * Monitor system health, performance, and compliance across Intune and Configuration Manager. * Troubleshoot complex issues related to device enrolment, policy application, software deployment, and patching. * Provide Tier 3 support for endpoint\-related incidents and escalations. * Produce and maintain technical documentation, runbooks, and architectural diagrams. * Work closely with infrastructure, security, and service desk teams to ensure cohesive endpoint operations. Contribute to endpoint management strategy, roadmap planning, and modernisation initiatives. * **Skills, Qualifications \& Experience** * Strong hands\-on experience with Microsoft Intune (Endpoint Manager) and Configuration Manager (SCCM). * Proven ability to manage large\-scale enterprise device environments. * Expertise in Windows client management, MDM/MAM, Autopilot, patch management, and compliance policies. * Solid understanding of Azure Active Directory, Conditional Access, and identity\-driven security. * Proficiency with PowerShell scripting. * Experience with Defender for Endpoint or similar endpoint security platforms. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills. * Initiative\-taking and solution\-oriented mindset. * Effective communication skills. * Comfortable working independently and collaboratively. Passion for modern endpoint management and continuous improvement. * **Experience:** * Demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and operating Intune and SCCM in large enterprises. * History of managing diverse device ecosystems (Windows, macOS, iOS, Android). * Experience in building and enforcing security baselines and compliance policies. * Experience with automation, provisioning (Autopilot), and patching workflows. Experience providing Tier 3 support and collaborating with cross\-functional teams. * **Education \& Certifications:** * Relevant IT or Computer Science degree or equivalent experience. Certifications such as Microsoft Endpoint Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator Associate, or comparable qualifications are a plus. * **Visa:** Irish/EU Citizen or Stamp 4 Visa holder. **About Us** As Ireland's largest IT Solutions Provider with a growing global footprint, ergo offers deep expertise across managed IT \& hardware services, cloud, applications \& software services, IT resourcing, and modern work solutions. Our recent technology awards reflect our commitment to delivering industry\-leading IT solutions, highlighting our team's dedication and expertise.

title: Energy Analyst
description: **Wind Analysis Specialist (Renewables)** Location: **Newbridge, Co. Kildare, Ireland** (Hybrid working model with blended **working from home for three days** and **two days in the office** ) Duration: **Permanent** Reporting to: **Engineering Manager** Our client is an Irish Semi‑State Organisation, headquartered in Newbridge, Co. Kildare, has evolved into a modern **Climate‑Action Enterprise** . It now focuses on **Renewable Energy Development** , rehabilitation, waste and resource recovery, and **Large‑Scale Environmental Restoration projects.** Its mission centres on supporting Ireland’s transition to a Climate‑Neutral Economy by 2050 through **Clean‑Energy Solutions** , Biodiversity Regeneration, and Sustainable Land Management. They are hiring a **Wind Analysis Specialist** . The purpose of this role is to support the **Engineering team** in an ongoingpipeline of **RenewableProjects** in termsof **Wind Analysis** and **Resource Assessment.** This role will involve working with the engineering **Team Designing Windfarms,** assessing **Site Constraints, WindProfiling, Turbine Site Optimisation** and engaging with internal/external stakeholders as required at a project level. The role will also involve completing **Wind Farm Layout Designs** , **GIS Mapping, Energy Yield Assessments,** and **Project Optimisation** to overcome constraints identified for projects in the planning phase of a development. It will ensure the successful delivery of key projects in the **Renewable** space. Success in this role will be measured by quality **Wind Layout Designs,** **Quality Resource Assessments** , timely delivery of projects, cost savings, improved team performance and contribution to the overall success of the department and organisation. The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: * **Windfarm Analysis:** * Produce **Windfarm Layouts** using **WindPro** or similar software like **Windfarmer** or **Openwind** * Working with the engineering team colleagues to optimise solutions to **Infrastructure Design.** * Assessing **GIS mapping** and constraints for the **Wind Farm, Solar Farm** , and other **Renewable Energy Projects.** * Contribute to various **Wind Farm Studies** including **Telecommunications,** **Noise, Shadow Flicker** and **Visual ImpactAssessments** to aid the development and operation of projects. * Produce **Solar Layouts** using **PVsyst** or similar software. * **Wind Appraisal Resource Assessment:** * Assist with the **Field Deployment** and **Monitoring** of remote sensing installations for resource assessment measurements for the project pipeline, including **Wind** ( **mast and LiDAR)** and **Solar Measurement.** * Checking, management and **Analysis** of recorded data as required across the **Project Portfolio** including initial **Project Energy Yield Estimates.** * Complete **Site Suitability Studies** to characterise sites and develop project layout options, including working with **external Consultants** to validate and modify as necessary. * **Analyse** and **Optimise Wind Resource** and **Energy Yield Predictions** for new, developing, and **Operational Wind Farms.** * **Site Condition Assessments** for **Pre\-Tender Information** and input for **Turbine Selection.** * Contribute to the procurement of external services related to **Turbine Supply Agreements, Wind Data Management, Resource Assessment, Site Suitability Assessments** and curtailment analysis(noise, shadow flicker)associated with company related projects. * Provide technical support for the due diligence of their investments in **Renewable Energy Projects** , including review of **Consultant Reports** and specification of third party analysis. * Prepare and/or review **Scope of Work** for third party services for project resource assessment and fully participate in the various stages of the associated procurement processes. Support the monitoring and evaluation of the performance of principle consultants with respect to their use on future projects. * Ensure that all works carried out under your remit comply with their Health, Safety and Environmental policies and all relevant EU and National policies and statutory requirements. * All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises. * Proactively participate in the **Performance Management** process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's objectives. **The Person:** The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: * Level 8 qualification required in engineering, science or relevant discipline for **Wind Analysis.** * Minimum four years relevant post\-graduate experience. * Experience using specialist software for **Wind Layout Design** such as **WindPro** is essential. * Experience conducting **Geospatial Analysis** and geoprocessing tasks using **Arcgis Pro,ǪGIS, Mapinfo** or similar. * Knowledge of **Data Analysis** using advanced **MS Excel, Matlab, Python, R** or similar. * Knowledge of using specialist software for **Solar Layout Design** such as **PVsyst** or similar desirable but not essential. * Strong analytical and IT skills. * Ability to communicate clearly with other specialists, professionals and management not from a technology background. * Good interpersonal and teamwork skills with capability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team. * Ability to balance attention to detail with engineering judgment. * Positive attitude, flexibility and willingness to tackle new areas of work beyond core skill. **Other Benefits Include:** Employer matched pension contributions They invest in their people and have a strong commitment to your development. Please send a copy of your CV to **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*

title: Entry Level - Site Reliability Engineer (Remote - Ireland)
description: **Summary** Yelp engineering culture is driven by our values: we’re a cooperative team that values individual authenticity and encourages creative solutions to problems. All new engineers deploy working code their first week, and we strive to broaden individual impact with support from managers, mentors, and teams. At the end of the day, we’re all about helping our users, growing as engineers, and having fun in a collaborative environment. Do you want to build and manage scaleable, self\-healing, globally\-distributed systems? Our Site Reliability engineers keep Yelp fast, available, and growing, connecting users to great local businesses. No matter how many times we get searched, scraped, scanned, spammed, pinged, paged, or queried, we keep our cool \- and keep the site running smoothly. We work in both the dev and systems worlds, implementing key parts of the core architecture and supporting devs as they try to do the same. We get to tackle interesting challenges that you can only find at the kind of scale that serves over 100 million users per month. You'll work to empower product teams and developers: at Yelp, spinning up infrastructure should always be a git commit and a code review away: automation and self\-service are at the core of what we do. This opportunity requires you to be located in the Republic of Ireland. We’d love to have you apply, even if you don’t feel you meet every single requirement in this posting. At Yelp, we’re looking for great people, not just those who simply check off all the boxes. **What You'll Do** * Work closely with engineers in supporting new features and services. * Build tools to monitor site stability and performance. * Help ensure the reliability and scalability of our infrastructure, while maintaining platform SLOs. * Troubleshoot site issues using industry\-leading tools like Splunk, Prometheus and OpenTelemetry. * Automate everything with Python, Puppet, Git, Jenkins, and Terraform and leveraging an extensive suite of AI tooling. * Develop custom tools when off\-the\-shelf solutions don’t work at our scale and contribute upstream to open source projects. * Participate in light on\-call rotations \- we have geographically distributed SRE teams for follow\-the\-sun support, which means nobody needs to be on\-call 24h a day! **What It Takes To Succeed** * Familiarity with Linux and an enthusiasm for learning more (we use Ubuntu but any distro is fine). * Command of your favorite modern programming language: Python, Ruby, Go, Java, C\+\+, etc. * Hands\-on experience with public cloud platforms (we use AWS, but Azure/GCP are fine). * Practical background in Infrastructure as Code and Configuration Management (e.g., Terraform, Puppet, Ansible, or similar). * Relevant experience with Linux containerisation and orchestration (e.g., Docker and Kubernetes). * A solid understanding of fundamental technologies like TCP/IP, HTTP (transport, serving and load\-balancing), and DNS, containerization \& workload orchestration or datastores and streaming technologies. * Exposure to monitoring concepts and best practices around performance, reliability and security. * Excellent communication and documentation skills. * An eagerness to ask questions, take initiative and learn everyday. **What You'll Get** * Full responsibility for projects from day one, a collaborative team, and a dynamic work environment. * Competitive salary, a pension scheme, and an optional employee stock purchase plan. * 25 days paid holiday (rising to 29 with service), plus one floating holiday. * €150 monthly reimbursement to help cover remote working expenses. * €86 caregiver reimbursement to support dependent care for families. * Private health insurance, including dental and vision. * Flexible working hours and meeting\-free Wednesdays. * Regular 3\-day Hackathons, bi\-weekly learning groups, and productivity spending to support and encourage your career growth. * Opportunities to participate in digital events and conferences. * €86 per month to use toward qualifying wellness expenses. * Quarterly team offsites. Closing *Yelp values diversity. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital or family status, veteran status, medical condition, disability, or any other protected status.* **Notice to Northern Ireland Applicants** *A Basic criminal background check via AccessNI is required for employment. Yelp complies with the AccessNI Code of Practice. Having a criminal record will not necessarily prevent a candidate from working with Yelp. Yelp will consider the nature of the position together with the circumstances and background of the candidate's offences or other information contained on a disclosure certificate. AccessNI's Privacy Policy is available here. Yelp's Criminal Background Check Policy is available upon request.* *Note: Yelp does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to any recruiting alias or employee. Yelp is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.* Recruiting and Applicant Privacy Notice

title: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Specialist

title: Executive Associate (Strategy, Data & Delivery)
description: The Executive Associate, DCEO Office (Operations \& Insights) is a high‑impact, early‑career role that blends executive support, data analysis, project delivery, and board‑level storytelling. Partnering directly with the DCEO, you will orchestrate the rhythm of the business, secure timely, high‑quality inputs from functions and service providers, and transform complex data into crisp narratives and decision materials. The role flexes with the CEO’s priorities week‑to‑week, ensuring momentum, clarity, and results. **Key Responsibilities** * Run the DCEO Rhythm of the Business: own the annual, quarterly, monthly, and weekly cadence, including inputs, deadlines, and deliverables. * Board \& ExComm reporting: shape storylines, synthesise metrics, and produce clear, visually compelling packs that “bring the requisite audience on a journey” without overloading figures. * Insight generation: translate operational and financial data into trends, risks, opportunities, and recommended actions. * Data pipeline coordination: secure timely, complete, and high‑quality data from functions and external service providers; resolve gaps and inconsistencies fast. * Presentation excellence: build executive‑level slides and visuals in PowerPoint, uphold a consistent design system and narrative structure. * Project management: plan, track, and deliver DCEO‑sponsored initiatives, manage scope and comms. * Stakeholder orchestration: align C\-Suite colleagues, Directors, Finance, HR, Medical, Clinical/Ops, IT, and external partners around DCEO priorities. * Decision support: prepare succinct decision memos (“one‑pagers”), options, trade‑offs, and clear recommendations. * Executive briefings: package pre‑reads and talking points for internal and external meetings; ensure pre‑wire with key stakeholders. * Follow‑through: capture meeting actions, their owners, and due dates, then drive to closure and surface slippage early. * Issue escalation: spot signals in sow downs or failures, escalate the right issues to the DCEO with context and proposed fixes. * Confidentiality \& governance: handle sensitive information with discretion, follow information‑security and approvals protocols. * Calendar and priority triage: protect DCEO focus time, sequence work to match shifting priorities. * Vendor/service‑provider management: set expectations, SLAs, and delivery quality for data and content inputs. * KPI / dashboard upkeep: maintain a concise “single source of truth” for top metrics and update cadence. * Change agility: rapidly re‑plan when priorities move, communicate impacts and next steps. * Process improvement: simplify reporting cycles and automate where practical (templates, checklists, light scripts). * Internal communications: draft professional crisp updates from DCEO office as required. * Knowledge management: maintain a searchable, version‑controlled repository of past packs, decisions, and templates. * Culture \& standards: model pace, professionalism, and high bar for quality across all deliverables. **Person Requirements** * Early‑career track record (typically 2–4 years) in one or more of: analytics, strategy/ops, PMO, management consulting, Human Resources or high‑performing EA/Chief‑of‑Staff‑associate roles. * Outstanding PowerPoint: visual storytelling, executive‑level slide craft, and narrative design. * Strong quantitative literacy: Excel/Sheets for analysis; comfort with BI outputs (Power BI/Tableau) and manipulating datasets. * Project delivery: plans, stakeholder management, and a reputation for closing actions. * Executive presence \& writing: concise briefs, decision memos, and clear meeting pre‑reads. * Stakeholder savvy: confident with senior leaders, can secure inputs without formal authority. * Bias for action: self‑starter, organised, and calm under shifting priorities and short notice. * High integrity \& discretion handling confidential information. **Desirable Requirements** * Exposure to board reporting or investor‑style narratives. * Power BI/Tableau basics; familiarity with SharePoint/OneDrive for structured content management. * PMP/PRINCE2, Agile foundations, or analytics/data‑viz coursework/certifications. * Experience working with external vendors/service providers and SLAs. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree (business, economics, analytics, engineering, or related) or equivalent practical experience. THE MATER PRIVATE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: Fabricator
description: Cental is a global supplier of fully designed, engineered, built and integrated modular and skid\-mounted solutions for the Data Centre, Utilities, Life Sciences, and Oil \& Gas markets. From our headquarters in Carlow, Ireland, we deliver comprehensive solutions for clients worldwide. Our people are the foundation of our success. With three manufacturing facilities and a talented team of engineers, designers, project managers, and tradespeople, our team manages every detail, ensuring seamless delivery from initial design to final installation. We value teamwork, respect, and continuous improvement, creating a collaborative, innovative environment where everyone can reach their potential. We also believe in celebrating achievements and recognising effort. From monthly quality and safety initiatives for breakfast treats and local tool vouchers, to company breakfasts, staff BBQs, and occasional outings, we bring people together to enjoy achievements and strengthen our team spirit. Growth and learning are central to Cental. Whether you’re joining as an apprentice, graduate, or experienced professional, we provide training and development opportunities to help you build a rewarding, long\-term career. If you’re passionate about excellence, innovation, and teamwork, join us and be part of a company that’s engineering solutions and building futures. Cental is seeking a Fabricator who will be responsible for cutting, shaping, assembling, and welding metal components to produce high‑quality steel structures, frames, and modular components in line with engineering drawings, quality standards, and safety requirements. The role supports high‑volume manufacturing in a fast‑paced environment, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and consistent product quality. **Key Responsibilities** * Interpret and work from technical drawings, blueprints, and fabrication plans. * Fabricate steel components using cutting, grinding, drilling, forming, and welding processes. * Assemble structural components ensuring accurate dimensions and tolerances. * Operate fabrication equipment such as: + MIG/Stick welding machines + Plasma cutters + Saws (band saw, chop saw) + Press brakes and rollers + Grinders and power tools * Conduct weld preparation, finishing, and quality checks. * Ensure welds meet internal quality standards and any relevant codes. * Inspect finished products for defects or deviations. * Complete rework where required. * Ensure compliance with quality procedures, ISO standards, and project specifications. * Adhere strictly to company safety policies, including PPE requirements. * Work collaboratively with engineering, production, and assembly teams. **Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience as a Fabricator in a manufacturing or engineering environment. * Strong ability to read and interpret fabrication and engineering drawings. * Skilled in MIG welding. * Competent in measuring, marking out, and working to tight tolerances. * Experience using fabrication workshop tools and machinery. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Finance Associate
description: **Title: Finance Associate** **Reports to: Finance Manager** **Duration: Full\-time and Permanent** We are currently looking for a Finance Associate to work in our Finance Department. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and close to 500 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. title: Finance Graduate Programme 2026
description: NYX Hotels is giving laidback luxury: we are THE destination! Our NYX team (we call them City Lovers!) is a diverse bunch of vibrant people, who are on a mission to make NYX the first\-choice for guests looking for a high\-end stay with a unique, urban feel. NYX Hotels is a part of the Leonardo Hotels portfolio. **Please note: Successful applicants will be considered for opportunities across multiple Leonardo Hotels locations in the UK and Ireland.** Just about to graduate or graduated last year and wondering what’s next? With over 57 hotels in the UK \& Ireland and over 300 worldwide, and award\-winning L\&D offerings, we have exciting opportunities to not only take part in our Finance Graduate Programme but also grow within an ever\-expanding company to achieve your career goals with the support of our L\&D programmes. **Finance Graduate Programme** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * This is a rotational programme covering all areas of the finance department such as accounting, management accounting, financial planning, analysis and much more! * At month 6, you will be enrolled on the HOSPA Qualification, allowing you to be Certified as AHOSPA. * Extensive personal and professional development is provided throughout the programme with training workshops, webinars and project work. * Fixed\-term contract with the aim of securing a permanent management role by the end of the programme. **What we are looking for?** * A passion for genuine hospitality * A proactive, positive and resilient attitude * A degree in a Finance related course * Ideally, you will have previously worked in an operational role in Hotels, Leisure or Tourism * You’ll be based in on hotel throughout the programme; flexibility on placement is best to ensure you get the most out of your programme **What’s in it for you?** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * Extensive personal and professional development * Support of industry professionals with a dedicated mentor along with a programme sponsor. **What's next?** * Apply online here today! * Applications close **June 2026** * We will be in touch by **July 2026** * You will take part in a 2\-step process – 1 assessment centre and if successful, a final interview with the Financial Controller of your placement hotel * Opportunities to start with us from August/September with the graduate programme commencing November **2026** Some of the perks our Finance Graduate Programme 2026 could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a Finance Graduate Programme 2026? At NYX Hotels, we look after our City Lovers just as well as we look after our guests. Once you’re part of the NYX team, you’re part of the Leonardo Hotels community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: Financial Accountant
description: **Overview** As a **Financial** **Accountant** at Linesight, you will work with highly experienced, culturally diverse, and passionate teams nurturing a strong social dynamic and a shared sense of achievement. You will gain vast industry experience that will open doors for you, extend your skillset and expand your perspective. We will empower, support, and enable you to take real responsibility in your role so that you can take charge of your career progression with us. For us lasting trusted relationships are everything, both with our teams and our clients. **The Opportunity** In this role you will: * Take ownership of the preparation of the monthly management accounts including profit and loss account, balance sheet and cashflow report for assigned entities * Assist with the external audit where required * Complete all assigned monthly balance sheet reconciliations * Complete regulatory reporting – VAT, RTD and VIES returns as required * Prepare year end files for audit and tax returns * Review and approve supplier payments * Support the budget and forecast process * Support the payroll process including reviews and posting payroll journals * Support junior members of the team, providing training and playing an active role in their development * Detailed understanding of accruals and prepayment accounting * Tangible and Intangible asset accounting * Bank Reconciliations * Management and Statutory account variance analysis * Detailed understanding of bad debt provisioning or AR reserve We would love to hear from you if you: * Are ACA/ACCA qualified with 1\-3 years post qualification industry experience * Adapt to new processes with agility and enthusiasm * Are detail oriented and quality focused * Excel in a collaborative and friendly team environment * Adapt to new processes with agility and enthusiasm * Thrive in a hands\-on environment focusing on innovation, resourcefulness, and velocity * Thrive on building strong relationships with your team and clients through a shared purpose and sense of openness and fun * Experience working within a multicurrency environment would be an advantage. All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually with video required. **About Us** Linesight is a highly successful global project and cost management consultancy that keeps clients coming back. And for that we have our people to thank. You see we’re not like the others. We’re different. Unique. It’s our fresh thinking and focus on what matters that has led to our evolving.
