Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * English language teaching: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * TESOL / CELTA or equivalent (preferred) Work authorisation: * Irlande (required) Work Location: In person

title: Systems Engineer/Dev Ops Eng, Managed Operations
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- AWS has successfully launched the European Sovereign Cloud (ESC), marking a significant development in Utility Computing (UC). To spearhead this initiative, we are actively seeking experienced systems engineers with a strong background in automation and operations. As part of the AWS Managed Operations team, you will play a pivotal role in building, operating and evolving operations and development teams dedicated to delivering high\-availability AWS services, including EC2, S3, Dynamo, Lambda, and Bedrock, exclusively for EU customers. For more information on ESC please check out our blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/in\-the\-works\-aws\-european\-sovereign\-cloud/ Your responsibilities will encompass overseeing the ongoing operations and expansion of the ESC, working closely with global AWS teams, and influencing the evolution of AWS services and technology. A typical day in this role involves collaborating with technology leaders, contributing to the enhancement of day\-to\-day operations, and ensuring continuous improvements in availability, reliability, latency, performance, and efficiency of the ESC. You will be required to occasionally participate in “on\-call” rotations to resolve incidents occurring out\-of\-hours. The overarching goal is to deliver scalable services and ensure a high\-availability experience for EU customers. If you are an experienced professional ready for a challenging and impactful opportunity, we invite you to join our efforts in operating and scaling a best\-in\-class development engineering and operations team that aligns with AWS' commitment to customer satisfaction and continual innovation. A day in the life Embark on a week filled with meaningful contributions to the operation and improvement of significant software systems. You dedicate a substantial portion of your time to carefully review the operational health of services within your team's responsibility. In the process, you diligently identify anomalies and craft actionable bug reports, aspiring to enhance the overall efficiency and performance of your systems. In addition to these responsibilities, you offer constructive feedback on change management documents and work earnestly to address your team's operational backlog. Through a collaborative effort, you strive to navigate challenges and ensure the seamless functionality of your systems. Additionally, you engage in the development and testing of scripts, hoping to provide practical solutions to enhance your workflows. Beyond the technical aspects, you assume a role as an educator, sharing insights on the complexities of the European Sovereign Cloud with service teams. It's a humbling experience for you to contribute to the collective knowledge of the team, fostering a culture of mutual understanding. This week encapsulates your commitment to continuous learning and improvement, acknowledging that every effort, no matter how small, contributes to the collective success of your team and the reliability of your software systems. In addition to these responsibilities, your position involves 24x7 on\-call responsibility. You work as a team to root\-cause issues and ensure your systems remain resilient and fault\-tolerant, underscoring your commitment to maintaining operational excellence. Eligibility requirement * Fluency in written and spoken English is required. * Candidate must be a national of an EU member state and residing in the EU to operate the AWS European Sovereign Cloud. * Amazon will provide relocation support for successful applicants relocating within the European Union. * Employees will participate in an On\-Call rota. About the team Utility Computing (UC) European Sovereign Cloud (ESC) is a part of AWS Utility Computing (UC). AWS Utility Computing (UC) provides product innovations — from foundational services such as Amazon’s Simple Storage Service (S3\) and Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2\), to consistently released new product innovations that continue to set AWS’s services and features apart in the industry. As a member of the UC organization, you’ll support the development and management of Compute, Database, Storage, Internet of Things (Iot), Platform, and Productivity Apps services in AWS. Within AWS UC, Managed Operations engineers engage with AWS customers who require specialized security solutions for their cloud services. Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Experience in Linux OS and network troubleshooting, or experience in networking administration and troubleshooting * Experience in Python, Perl, or another scripting language * Experience in Systems engineering, site reliability engineering, building and operating systems at scale * This role requires you to be a national of an EU member state * Able to lead the creation, revision, and/or improvement of standard operational procedures (SOPs) and driving operational best practices. **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Experience with monitoring frameworks (such as CloudWatch, Datadog, Grafana, Elastic or similar). * Experience actively mentoring junior engineers and working cross\-organizationally and leading strategic team efforts requiring work from multiple team members * Experience operating 24x7 high\-availability, distributed software applications and performance tuning software applications and optimizing fleet utilization * Experience with Infrastructure as Code, (such as CDK, CloudFormation, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, or similar) * Experience with CI/CD pipelines, DevOps practices, and Generative AI technologies, including automated deployment, configuration management, continuous integration workflows, prompt engineering, model deployment, and AI\-powered automation tools Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Talent Acquisition Specialist
description: Our client is a leading Irish organisation operating within the financial and professional services sector. With a strong market presence and a long\-established reputation, the organisation supports a diverse client base including corporates, institutions, and private clients. They are known for their collaborative culture, focus on excellence, and commitment to developing their people. This is an opportunity to join a high\-performing environment where you can contribute meaningfully while continuing to grow your career. **About the Role** An opportunity has arisen for a Recruiter to join a busy and dynamic HR team on a 12\-month fixed\-term contract. This role plays a key part in attracting and hiring high\-quality talent across the organisation. You will manage end\-to\-end recruitment processes, partner closely with hiring managers, and contribute to delivering a best\-in\-class candidate experience. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage full\-cycle recruitment including sourcing, screening, interviewing, and offer management * Proactively source candidates through job boards, social media, and networking channels * Partner with internal stakeholders to understand hiring needs and develop effective sourcing strategies * Support diversity and inclusion initiatives through recruitment practices * Manage graduate/internship hiring campaigns through to offer stage * Track and analyse recruitment metrics to drive continuous improvement * Collaborate with internal teams to enhance employer branding and candidate engagement * Support the implementation and optimisation of HR systems (e.g. HRIS) * Ensure a high\-quality candidate experience throughout the recruitment lifecycle * Build and manage relationships with external recruitment partners where required * Attend career fairs and networking events to attract talent * Stay up to date on market trends and recruitment best practices * Support ad hoc HR and recruitment projects as needed * Ensure compliance with relevant policies and regulatory requirements **About You** * Degree in HR, Business, or a related discipline * 1–2 years’ experience in recruitment (in\-house or agency), ideally supporting multiple functions * Experience within financial services, professional services, or a regulated environment is advantageous * Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills * Experience using recruitment data and metrics to inform decision\-making * Exposure to HR systems or HRIS implementation is desirable * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Strong sourcing capability, including use of social and digital channels * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * High attention to detail and strong organisational skills * Proactive, self\-motivated, and a strong team player * Passion for delivering an excellent candidate experience **What’s on Offer** * Competitive salary and benefits package (including bonus and pension) * Hybrid working model * Health and wellbeing supports, including Employee Assistance Programme * Learning and development opportunities to support career growth * Inclusive and collaborative working environment * Opportunity to gain experience in a well\-established and reputable organisation

title: Tax Analyst
description: A well‑established Irish organisation with a significant domestic footprint is looking to hire a **Tax Analyst** to join its in‑house tax function. This is a broad, hands‑on role offering exposure across **corporation tax, VAT and operational tax matters** , with real scope to influence processes and work closely with stakeholders across the business. **ABOUT THE ROLE** * Preparing and reviewing corporate tax, VAT and statutory returns * Analysing technical tax issues and delivering practical solutions * Assessing legislative changes and their commercial impact * Advising stakeholders on tax matters linked to business activity * Maintaining and enhancing tax controls, processes and documentation * Driving process improvements, automation and efficiencies **ABOUT THE PERSON** * At least three years’ experience in tax. * Qualified Tax Advisor and Qualified Accountant (ACA/ACCA/CTA). * Strong communication skills and the ability to interact confidently with various stakeholders. * Ability to multitask and prioritise important tasks.

title: Tax Graduate
description: Tax Graduate, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Responsibilities Of The Role** * Assisting with the preparation and timely filing of tax returns and payments across all jurisdictions the Group operates. * Assisting in the preparation of tax materials for the purposes of the preparation of financial statements. * Assisting in the preparation of materials to be submitted to Tax Authorities as requested. * Researching tax implications for entries into new markets and specific transactions. * Liaising with Management and staff in relation to issues which arise in all jurisdictions in which the Group operates. * Assisting with the preparation of company secretarial filings in all jurisdictions in which the Group operates. * Develop your technical knowledge through reading, preparation for and passing of prescribed professional exams. * Providing support to ad\-hoc tax or commercial projects as they arise. * Ad\-hoc travel to overseas jurisdictions for the purposes of meeting and supporting local construction projects from a tax perspective. * Taking part in and engaging with the Mercury Graduate Programme in a meaningful way. * All other duties associated with this role **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& co\-ordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable Criteria** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: Technician

Technical Degree (2 years) 3\-5\+ years experience Our Differences make our Performance At Air Liquide, we are committed to build a diverse and inclusive workplace that embraces the diversity of our employees, our customers, patients, community stakeholders and cultures across the world. We strongly believe a diverse organization opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to innovate by living our fundamentals, acting for our success and creating an engaging environment in a changing world.

title: Trade Mark Attorney
description: **Position:** Trade Mark Attorney **Team:** Intellectual Property **The Firm:** Mason Hayes Curran LLP is a leading Irish law firm based in Dublin with representative offices in San Francisco, New York and London. The firm has been the fastest growing law firm in Ireland over the last decade. We are a full\-service business law firm with a particular focus on the Energy, Technology, Financial Services, Built Environment, Healthcare, Life Sciences and Charities sectors. **The Role:** Our award winning Intellectual Property team is seeking a motivated Trade Mark Attorney to join our growing team. This is a new role due to continuing expansion of our trade marks practice. We manage global trade mark portfolios for market leading Irish and international clients covering a range of industries including food and beverage, technology, life sciences, fashion, consumer goods, energy and real estate. They trust us to advise on trade mark strategy and manage their global trade mark portfolios. The role will include: * Preparing and filing trade marks with the EUIPO, IPOI and WIPO. * Advising on trade mark filings and prosecution, including dealing with formalities issues. * Managing our trade marks database. * Attending to trade mark renewals, assignment recordals, changes of name and similar issues. * Managing Irish, EU and global trade mark portfolios. * Liaising with overseas associates on trade mark issues. * Conducting trade mark searches and managing trade mark watches. * Preparing and filing trade mark oppositions, cancellations and revocations. * Assisting with trade mark due diligence. This role will suit an ambitious self\-starter, who works well in a team. Strong analytical and organisational skills as well as attention to detail will be key attributes for this role. This is a great opportunity for someone to make the role their own, working closely with our IP partners and lawyers. **Candidate Requirements:** * Ideally a qualified Irish Trade Mark Agent or European Trade Mark and Designs Attorney. * 2\+ years’ experience in the area of trade marks. * Experience with WebTMS (IPPO) or similar trade mark database. * Experience in undertaking trade mark clearance searches. **The Team** Our dedicated top tier Intellectual Property team specialises in protecting, capitalising and maximising our clients’ investments in their IP. All of our lawyers are also Trade Mark Attorneys and work in all areas of trade mark law. We are a close knit, collegiate team and work on varied, interesting and high profile matters. We are regularly top ranked with legal directories and were pleased to be recognised as Ireland Trade Mark Prosecution Firm of the Year at the Managing IP Awards 2025 among other awards. **Why MHC?** * Excellent remuneration and benefits package; pension, healthcare bonus. * Enviable client base, exciting work and strong career progression opportunities. * Friendly, supportive and informal working atmosphere. * Flexible/hybrid working arrangements. * Focus on employee involvement, wellbeing and success. * Social events, clubs and initiatives (Green Team, Working on Wellness, subsidised fitness classes, MHC RISE, Diversity, Sports Social and Art Committee). * MHC knowledge and education programme to facilitate professional development. So if you like challenge, value autonomy, work well within a team, enjoy partnering with senior business stakeholders and love working with top notch clients then apply to join us @MASON, HAYES CURRAN.

title: Trainee Accountant 2026 (Dublin 4)
description: **Position: Trainee Accountant** 2026 **Location: Ballsbridge, Dublin 4\.** **Start Date: 2026** **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** This is a full time \& office\-based role in **Dublin 4\.** The role would suit a recent Business/Commerce graduate with an interest pursuing a career in accounting. The successful candidate will enter our accountancy training program to achieve ACCA qualification or equivalent. **Role** * Exposure to Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable and General Accounts / Year end audit work * Processes purchase invoices from P.O. stage to payment * Reconcile processed work by verifying entries and comparing system reports to balances * Ensure all invoices/expense are fully and correctly costed * Maintain accounts payable ledgers by verifying and posting account transactions * Verify vendor accounts by reconciling monthly statements and related transactions * Generate sales invoices and account statements * Assist with cash control and resolve customer queries * Maintain accounts receivable ledgers and records * Liaises with auditors and complete bank reconciliations **Requirements** * No experience necessary * Excellent IT/Communications skills essential * Ability to work as part of a multi\-functional team * Committed to pursuing an accounting qualification (ACCA or equivalent) * ACCA or equivalent Examination Exemptions (preferrable but not essential) **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. title: Trainee Industrial Painter – Manufacturing
description: **Trainee Industrial Painter \- Manufacturing** We are recruiting a trainee painter to join our paint shop team. This role is ideal for someone practical, physically fit and interested in learning a skilled trade in manufacturing. No previous experience is required. Attitude and willingness to learn are more important than experience. You will be trained in industrial paint systems used in heavy manufacturing, working alongside experienced team members and with support from a world\-class paint supplier. This is not a quick course. Learning to paint properly takes time. Over approximately three years you will gain solid industrial painting experience, a valuable skill used across engineering, transport and manufacturing industries. This role suits someone who enjoys practical work and wants to learn a skilled trade rather than work in an office. **What The Job Involves** Preparing steel equipment for painting, including time working in the blast shop Assisting with spray painting and finishing Learning industrial coating systems and application techniques Maintaining high quality standards in the paint shop **What We Are Looking For** Physically fit and comfortable with hands\-on manual work A good attitude and willingness to learn Reliable and punctual Interested in developing a practical trade **What We Offer** Structured on\-the\-job training Support from experienced team members Training with a leading international paint supplier The opportunity to build a long\-term skill in manufacturing **Salary** Starting rate from €15\.00 per hour during the initial training period Pay increases on a sliding scale as skills and experience develop Fully trained industrial painters typically earn €18 \- €22 per hour depending on experience Portnahinch, Portarlington, R32CD40 Monday to Friday 8\.30\-5\.00

title: Transportation Engineer
description: **About The Roles** DBFL are currently seeking to recruit Traffic and Transportation Professionals. Our ever\-increasing project folio and successful positioning on frameworks with local authorities, the National Transport Authority Transport Infrastructure Ireland has generated positions in our offices in Dublin, Cork Waterford. We have a significant and expanding workload in the traffic and transportation planning sector. Current projects include: * The development and preparation of Transport Strategies/LTPs for large towns and Local Areas * Leading multidisciplinary design teams and partners in developing transport networks for new towns, residential commercial developments in accordance with DMURS * The design appraisal of Active Travel schemes (walking cycling) in urban and rural environments including greenways (preliminary and detailed) * The design of a number of significant bus priority schemes * The design of various traffic management schemes (including environmental improvement and traffic calming schemes of towns and villages) * Providing assistance/advice to other departments in DBFL (roads/highway design, street network design in accordance with DMURS, car park design, service access strategies) * Providing transport related advice and services to support high profile clients in the development of large residential, commercial and educational developments including the development of major new towns and Strategic Development Zones DBFL has excellent relationships with the many clients that we work with on a regular basis. We work on the principle that we are the first company that they think of when they require transportation consultancy services. In order to facilitate the continued expansion of this section, DBFL urgently requires Transportation Engineers Planners with a minimum of 3 years experience. **Key Roles And Responsibilities** Reporting to an Associate Director, successful candidates pending experience will display the following key traits and skills:\- * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Strong numerical skills and problem\-solving abilities * At least 3 years transport modelling experience * A relevant engineering/planning degree and either hold or are working towards Chartered status * Ideally hold a relevant postgraduate or Masters qualification * Experience with the NTA RMS (Desired) or variable demand modelling * Experience using any one or more of Cube, SATURN, Aimsun, Paramics, Vissum, Vissim, EMME ( 2 years exp with Saturn, Paramics, and 1 year exp CUBE, desired) * Excellent analytical skills to inform decision making, including spreadsheet, data analysis (good Data analysis) * Use and application of GIS Techniques to transport modelling outputs and analysis * Experience working with complex datasets * Qualitative quantitative research skills **Desired Qualifications And Requirements** * Display a good understanding of design principles, national / local authority standards, technical guidance documents such as DMURS, DMRB, TII SRW etc * Have multi\-disciplinary design co\-ordination experience and be able to present design solutions effectively holistically within an overall project team * Ability to mentor junior staff in developing their own technical expertise. * Good planning and organisational skills. * Familiar with the preparation of proposals and tender submissions * A current driver’s licence is desirable **What We Offer** * Significant investment in your career development. * Competitive market salary * Pension matched up to 5% * Generous Annual Leave Allowance (basic allowance 23 days) * Additional service recognition leave * Hybrid Working * Tax Saver Scheme Bike to Work Scheme. * Annual Professional Institution Subscriptions paid * Wellbeing support including access to an independent Employee Assistance Scheme. * A working culture that is diverse, inclusive and looks after your wellness at work. * Company Social Committee which organises many social events throughout the year. * Continuous technical learning paths through a wide range of activities, knowledge sharing platforms, seminars and workshops, lunch and learn presentations and other events that can support your career growth. **CPD Accreditation** We are a certified CPD Accredited Employer with Engineers Ireland (EI) , recognising that the company can only grow and develop through its people. Our employees are our major asset and as an EI CPD Accredited Employer we are fully committed to raising and maintaining employee competency levels in order to develop a workforce that is encouraged to create and innovate. **Equal Opportunities** At DBFL, we are proud to be a Gold Accredited Investors in Diversity award recipient, reflecting our ongoing commitment to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We believe that a variety of perspectives drives innovation and excellence, and we actively seek to create an environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their unique talents. Our dedication to diversity, equity, and inclusion is not just a policy, but a core value. DBFL is a forward\-thinking engineering consultancy and an equal opportunities employer. Availability: ASAP. Locations: Dublin, Cork Waterford Salary: Negotiable depending on experience.

title: Treasury Operations Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up on our Treasury Team. Reporting to the Treasury Associate Director, you will play a key role in providing Treasury support to the wider Davy business. Specific responsibilities include cash positioning and forecasting, funding, liquidity management, FX risk management and analysis and payments support. **In The Role, The Successful Candidate Will** * Overseeing the cash positioning and funding requirements for the business. * Monitor and reporting on any regulatory liquidity requirements. * Monitor FX risk exposures \- ensuring market trades are executed when needed. * Monitor and review FX rates. * Payment processing \- funding transfers, client payments, 3rd party payments and FX settlements. * Ensure all Treasury cash transactions are posted in the relevant ERP system. * Preparation and circulation of treasury reporting. * Develop and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders. * Provide cover within the department as required. * Work with Risk and Compliance to understand and improve on the existing Risk and Compliance frameworks associated with Treasury. * Support and assist with Treasury related projects. **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * Relevant 3rd level degree in Business, Finance or a related discipline. * Good communication and interpersonal skills. * Problem solving skills. * Accuracy and attention to detail. * Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to work under tight deadlines. * Strong processing skills and an eye for detail. * Proven team player with a flexible attitude towards work. * Past experience in a Treasury role an advantage, but not essential. **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal - FTC
description: Veterinary Practitioner – Small Animal Shenick Veterinary Hospital, Skerries Full\-time or Part\-time, Fixed Term Contract (6 months minimum) Salary commensurate with experience level A Flexible Small Animal Role That Grows With You If you’re looking for a small animal role that genuinely adapts to how you want to work, this could be the opportunity you’ve been waiting for. Shenick Veterinary Hospital is welcoming a Veterinary Surgeon to join our friendly, progressive team on a full\-time or part\-time fixed\-term contract (minimum six months), with a start date from April 2026\. With a salary of up to €55,000 DOE, this role is designed to offer variety, balance and real clinical satisfaction. Shape the Role Around Your Strengths This is a true first\-opinion GP position, but with flexibility at its core. Whether you’re an experienced clinician looking to focus more heavily on surgery or develop a particular clinical interest, or a confident junior vet comfortable with basic medicine and routine surgery who’s keen to progress, we’re keen to talk. We’re open to shaping the role around your experience, interests and long\-term goals. Your day\-to\-day work will include consultations, routine procedures, dentals and medical case work\-ups, all within a supportive environment that gives you the time and resources to work cases up properly. If surgery is your passion, we’re very open to building a stronger surgical focus into the role. Work\-Life Balance That’s Actually Respected One of the standout features of this role is the lifestyle it offers. There’s no out\-of\-hours or on\-call work, no bank holidays, and a predictable rota that keeps your evenings your own. The role is based on a 40\-hour week (with part\-time options available), worked Monday to Saturday between 8\.30am and 6\.30pm , with just one Saturday in three ( 9\.30am – 4\.00pm ). A Modern Hospital With a Strong Support Network Shenick Veterinary Hospital is well\-equipped with digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, an in\-house laboratory and hydrotherapy facilities. As part of a wider group that includes clinics in Balbriggan and Stamullen, you’ll benefit from access to a broad clinical network of experienced vets, including certificate holders. This allows for internal referrals, collaboration and excellent professional support. On site, you’ll be joining a close\-knit team of vets, nurses, a clinical director and dedicated support staff who value teamwork, communication and quality care. Who We’d Love to Hear From You’ll be a VCI\-registered Veterinary Surgeon, confident in routine consultations, case work\-ups and standard surgeries. While experience is preferred, this role could also suit a capable junior vet ready to take the next step in a busy, well\-supported first\-opinion practice. Rewarding You Properly Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package (pro rata for fixed\-term contracts/part\-time), including: Work Life Balance * 22 days Annual Leave * Birthday Leave (paid day off on your birthday) Focus on Employee Wellbeing * Paid Maternity Leave Package * Paid Paternity Leave * Cycle to Work Scheme * Full access to EAP and Wellbeing Centre * Paid Bereavement Leave * Company Sick Pay Development * €1300 annual CPD allowance with 5 days paid CPD days pro rata * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Career Progression Opportunities, both clinical and non\-clinical * Care Fund – Annual contribution of €1,100 to use on a case of your choice to ensure a successful outcome with your patient Professional Memberships * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Annual Pay Reviews * Pawesome Benefits \- reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with hundreds of retailers * Employer Pension Contributions * Free Flu Inoculations * Discounted Veterinary Fees * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQ\+, Wellbeing Champions, Nurses Committee, Clinical Board and many more) Let’s Talk If you’re looking for a small animal role that offers flexibility, strong support and the chance to shape your work around what you do best, we’d love to hear from you. Tell us how you like to work, where you want to grow, and let’s see how this role could fit you. €0 \- €60,000 a year Up to €60,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: WPM Ireland Module Process Engineer
description: **Job Details:** ================ **Job Description:** -------------------- The Wafer Packaging Manufacturing (WPM) SORT Test Engineering Organization in Leixlip is looking for motivated Module Process Engineer and Pack Ship/Integration Engineer to support its operations. The engineers in this team work with worldwide and local Intel teams to sustain and improve product, process and equipment health in the automated wafer test process and to enhance the quality, reliability and manufacturing of the overall test operation. The successful candidates will have the opportunity to influence and optimize the test and processing environment for some of the most advanced CPU and System\-On\-A\-Chip (SOC) products in the market. They will gain an understanding of Intel's chip architectures, test methods and processing flow in an environment that fosters and rewards technical innovation. **Responsibilities** include but are not limited to * Continuous technical learning about the Intel's Test Platforms \- incl. Semiconductor logic tester, 300mm wafer handler, probe card interface, test program and SW applications / embedded controllers, Integration flows and Sort systems. * Working with cross functional teams on investigation and debug of test platform failures \- Electrical, Process, Mechanical, Optical, S/W. * Containment and disposition of production material impacted by such failures (production material \= ICs under test). * Data mining (writing scripts to extract data) and data analysis of test results and tool logs to early\-detect performance issues with the Test Platforms. * Statistical analysis of IC parametric test\-results across the fleet of test equipment to detect discrepant equipment. * Investigation of parametric signals and correlate to test circuitry or mechanical/optical system \- to isolate failing H/W. * Provide a point of escalation for technicians experiencing problems in their process/equipment debug activities. * Coach technicians on debug activities \- document investigations and creation of debug content (instruction manuals and videos) for the technicians to improve performance of repair team. * Project managing new\-product readiness \- new test\-methods \- equipment upgrades/conversions and debug of test marginalities on new products. * Work with other engineering groups to improve overall performance of testing. * Managing the spares portfolio for the equipment and coordinating spares repair loops. * Manage Equipment performance, availability and can troubleshoot failures in a timely manner to ensure availability goals are met. * Develop scripts and processes to optimize test activities \- often in collaboration of other international Intel sites. * Collaborate with Intel sites internationally on the continuous improvement of the Test Environments to stay ahead of latest technology demands (releasing, testing, contributing content to new products and test capabilities) \- Scripts, procedures , debug methods. * Continuous learning about Intel products, IC test methodologies, new languages, and debug methods \- applying this knowledge in the debug of evolving product signals. * Understanding and improvement of Sort Integration and Shipping systems. * Understanding of Sort Systems and Supply Chain operations. **Qualifications:** ------------------- **Qualifications** : * A minimal qualification of honors degree (NFQ Level 8\) in Electronics, Mechatronics, Electrical, Computer Engineering, Applied Physic\\Skills or a related STEM field. * Must be a fast learner capable of researching new topics. * Must have the ability to solve problems independently. * Must also be a good communicator and capable of working as part of a team as often the problems involve several groups (S/W, H/W and process). * The ability to project plan and prioritize your work is essential. * Some aptitude for scripting/programming \- with some practical experience of any language \- Python/C\#/SQL... or similar. * Data analysis to solve problems (Pivots, SQL, Power Bi and JMP.) would be an advantage. * Practical experience of debug and problem solving under time constraints. * Moderate knowledge of statistics. * An understanding of micro\-electronics / Semiconductor physics is helpful. * Understanding of AI and willingness to implement AI into Sort processes is an advantage. **Other Criteria:** * Candidate must also be able to commit to travel on international assignments if required. **Job Type:** ------------- College Grad **Shift:** ---------- Shift 1 (Ireland) **Primary Location:** --------------------- Ireland, Leixlip **Additional Locations:** ------------------------- **Business group:** ------------------- Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement:** ---------------------- All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. **Position of Trust** --------------------- N/A **Work Model for this Role** This role will require an on\-site presence. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change. *

title: Wind Analyst – Layout Design
description: **Wind Analyst – Layout Design** **About the Company** We are currently working with one of Ireland’s leading energy companies who focus on the project management of large programs related to grid, power, transmission and distribution and renewable energy. They are currently looking to recruit a Wind Analyst with experience in design in to work on some of Ireland’s most essential projects. This client is one of Europe’s leading names in energy and engineering and carry a reputation of quality and innovation in the areas of Renewable Energy. **About the Position** These projects are related to renewable energy projects. * Produce windfarm layouts using WindPro, or similar software such as Openwind * Working with the engineering team colleagues to optimise solutions to infrastructure design. * Assessing GIS mapping and constraints for the wind farm, solar farm, and other renewable energy projects. * Contribute to various wind farm studies including telecommunications, noise, shadow flicker and visual impact assessments to aid the development and operation of projects. * Produce solar layouts using PVsyst or similar software. * Prepare and/or review scope of work for third party services for project resource assessment and fully participate in the various stages of the associated procurement processes. **Experience/Requirements** For this role you are required to have * 5 years experience in renewable energy * Substation and power knowledge. * Experience conducting geospatial analysis and geoprocessing tasks using Arcgis Pro, QGIS, Mapinfo or similar. * Experience with WindPro, Openwind or Windfarmer * Knowledge of data analysis using advanced MS Excel, Matlab, Python, R or similar. **Remuneration Package** The client offers an excellent package. They also offer pension, healthcare and a bonus structure. **Contact** For more information on this position or other engineering roles, please contact Cathal on 01\-5927850 or hit the apply button below to send your cv. Alternatively, contact him directly via email:
